title: Traditional Irrigation Methods
description: Traditional irrigation methods are simple, low-cost techniques used by farmers before modern irrigation systems. These methods depend on human or animal power and natural water sources like wells, ponds, and rivers.

methods.name: Moat (Pulley System)
methods.description: Water is drawn from wells using a rope and bucket. It is pulled manually or with the help of animals.

methods.name: Dhekli
methods.description: A long wooden lever system used to lift water. One side has a bucket, and the other acts as a counterweight. Requires less effort compared to the moat system.

methods.name: Chain Pump
methods.description: Consists of a rotating chain with small buckets attached. Buckets lift water continuously from the source. Can be operated manually or by animals.

methods.name: Rahat (Persian Wheel)
methods.description: A large wheel with buckets attached around it. Operated by animals like oxen. Provides continuous water supply to fields.

features: Low cost and easy to construct
Less efficient compared to modern methods
conclusion: Traditional irrigation methods played an important role in early agriculture and are still used in rural areas where modern irrigation facilities are not available.
